# AISS — Agent Identity and Signature Standard

> Cryptographic trust primitive for autonomous AI agents.
> Open standard · MIT License · No blockchain · No central authority

## What is AISS

AISS (Agent Identity and Signature Standard) defines the cryptographic foundation
for making autonomous AI agent actions verifiable, portable, and tamper-evident.

It is an open protocol specification — not a product, not a service.
Any system can implement it. Any agent can adopt it.

## Core primitives

- **Identity** — Deterministic agent ID derived from an Ed25519 public key (RFC §5–6)
- **Event chain** — Signed, hash-linked, append-only history (RFC §7–9)
- **Fork resolution** — Deterministic canonical chain selection (RFC §10)
- **A2A trust** — Agent-to-agent handshake with co-signed events (RFC §16)

## The problem AISS solves

Agent systems today produce logs that can be modified, lack cryptographic proof
of authorship, and rely on classical signatures vulnerable to quantum attacks.

AISS makes agent history independently verifiable — without access to the
original infrastructure, without a central registry, without blockchain.

## Cryptographic algorithms

- AISS-1: Ed25519 (RFC 8032) + SHA-256 + RFC 8785 canonicalization
- AISS-2: Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid (NIST FIPS 204, post-quantum)

## Quick integration

```python
pip install aiss
```

```python
from aiss import AgentIdentity

agent = AgentIdentity.create()
event = agent.stamp("decision_made", {"action": "approved", "confidence": 0.97})
assert agent.verify(event)
print(event["hash"])   # tamper-evident proof
```

## Event structure (AISS-1.0)

```json
{
  "version": "AISS-1.0",
  "agent_id": "3gFw2S1NT6dzo9vTPQ6JNWEwYFysZn8F",
  "timestamp": 1771845244,
  "nonce": "d363c917-ca77-4c63-94d4-c1f5f93261d2",
  "payload": { "event_type": "decision_made", "action": "approved" },
  "previous_hash": "27c5e890fa306d60...",
  "signature": "eA3oT793bV/hJnDX..."
}
```

## Compliance relevance

AISS provides the cryptographic mechanisms required by:
- EU AI Act Art. 12 — inviolable logging for high-risk AI systems
- SOC 2 CC6.6 — tamper-evident audit trails
- NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.5 — traceability of agent actions
- GDPR Art. 5.1.f — integrity of personal data processing records
- HIPAA §164.312 — immutable audit controls

Non-normative mapping. AISS does not provide compliance by itself.

## PCP — Proof of Continuity Protocol

AISS is Layer 1 of the Proof of Continuity Protocol (PCP):

```
AISS      — identity · event chain · fork resolution · A2A
PiQrypt   — reference implementation (monitoring, governance, certified exports)
```

AISS is independent. PiQrypt is one implementation.

## Specification

- Full RFC: https://github.com/piqrypt/aiss-standard/blob/main/docs/RFC_AISS_v2.0.md
- Conformance: https://github.com/piqrypt/aiss-standard/blob/main/CONFORMANCE.md
- GitHub: https://github.com/piqrypt/aiss-standard
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/aiss/

## Reference implementation

PiQrypt (Python): https://piqrypt.com
Adds: Vigil monitoring · TrustGate governance · certified exports · 9 framework bridges

## IP

Protocol concepts deposited via e-Soleau (INPI France):
- DSO2026006483 — 19 February 2026
- DSO2026009143 — 12 March 2026
